content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter Weekly Report (Apr 5 - 11, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
Development activity for the week of April 5–11, 2026, centered on improving cross-platform developer experience and environment stability. The primary focus was resolving compatibility hurdles for Windows users, specifically addressing file system conflicts that hindered repository maintenance. This effort ensures a more seamless onboarding and development workflow for contributors working across diverse operating systems.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Environment Compatibility
*   **Windows Git Compatibility:** The team initiated a fix to resolve issues where PGlite `:memory:` artifacts were causing git checkout blocks on Windows systems. This effort, tracked in [#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter/pull/25), aims to eliminate environment-specific friction and streamline the local development setup.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
*   **Closed Issues:** No issues were closed during this period.
*   **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened this week. The project currently has no active issues listed, with the primary focus remaining on the resolution of the pending pull request regarding environment compatibility.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Collaboration this week was focused on the technical refinement of the repository's environment configuration. Pull request [#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter/pull/25) remains the central point of activity, currently awaiting review to finalize the fix for Windows-based git checkout blocks. There were no other significant community discussions or new contributor activities reported during this timeframe.
